title: Refused --apply payload enumerates copy_lib/copy_helper blast radius (host_items)
action: In applyPlan() (onboard_bee.mjs ~:2388-2397), when --apply is refused on a blocked skill sync AND the aggregate refusal is forceable:true, attach host_items to the refusal response: plan.filter(({action}) => action === "copy_lib" || action === "copy_helper"), order preserved, verbatim from the already-computed plan — never recomputed (advisor finding 1). Forceable with zero lib/helper drift -> host_items: [] (present and empty). OMIT the field entirely for unknown-version, mixed-unforceable, and blocked_no_source refusals. Placement: top-level sibling beside skills in the public refusal payload; thread through BOTH applyPlan() and main()'s apply-refusal emission (~:2839-2856) (advisor finding 5). Do NOT add scope/target fields to host items — lib/helper paths are always repo-root-relative (advisor finding 7). Tests in test_onboard_bee.mjs, following the 10v three-step shape (:3260-3328) but STRENGTHENED per advisor findings 2-4: (a) compare exact normalized {action, path} arrays across dry-run plan, refused-apply host_items, and forced-apply applied set — never a permissive subset check (10v's :3311 discards fields and allows subsets; do not copy that); (b) the fixture must exercise BOTH action classes — seed top-level template helpers as well as templates/lib modules, discovered via filesystem enumeration (readdirSync), never a maintained filename list (critical-patterns: fixture list rot); (c) add a negative assertion to an existing unknown-version test: host_items absent AND force still refuses; (d) add a forceable-empty case asserting host_items: []. Do not change guard logic, block conditions, or what a forced apply executes. Do not hand-edit .claude/skills or .agents/skills projections. Legacy-global refresh items are OUT of scope (separate friction filed).

truths: refused forceable --apply response enumerates every pending copy_lib/copy_helper path in host_items, order-preserving, verbatim from the pending plan
non-forceable refusal (unknown version, blocked_no_source) carries NO host_items field and force still refuses
forceable refusal with zero lib/helper drift carries host_items: [] (present, empty)
forced apply touches exactly the previewed set — asserted as exact normalized {action,path} array equality across dry-run plan, refused host_items, and forced applied, no subset tolerance
test fixture exercises both copy_lib AND copy_helper classes, seeded via filesystem discovery
guard decision logic and applied-plan semantics are behavior-identical
host items carry no scope/target fields
artifacts: onboard_bee.mjs refusal payload with host_items threaded through applyPlan() and main()
strengthened three-step test + unknown-version negative + forceable-empty case in test_onboard_bee.mjs
prohibitions: no changes to hostLibDowngradeBlock decision logic or block conditions
no recomputation of the plan at refusal time — filter the existing array
no hand-kept expected-path or helper filename fixture lists
no permissive subset assertion copied from 10v :3311
no scope/target fields on host items
no legacy-global enumeration in this cell

trace.outcome: Refused --apply payload (and its dry-run twin) now enumerates the copy_lib/copy_helper blast radius as host_items: present+populated when forceable with pending drift, present+empty when forceable with zero drift, omitted entirely when non-forceable (unknown version/blocked_no_source). Filtered verbatim from the already-computed plan, no scope/target fields, guard logic untouched.
trace.verify_output: PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 1 (case-insensitive-fs check skips on /tmp, pre-existing/unrelated). New/strengthened assertions all ok: unknown-version refusal carries NO host_items field; unknown-version refusal via --force-downgrade also carries no host_items; host-items: plan mode reports blocked_downgrade (forceable); host-items: fixture exercises BOTH copy_lib and copy_helper action classes; host-items: dry-run plan enumerates exactly the expected copy_lib/copy_helper set, order-preserved; host-items: refused apply (no --force-downgrade) still reports blocked_downgrade; host-items: refused apply's host_items is EXACT normalized {action,path} array equality with the dry-run plan (no subset tolerance, unlike 10v :3311); host-items: host items carry NO scope/target fields (advisor finding 7); host-items: forcing actually applies; host-items: forced apply touches EXACTLY the previewed host_items set - exact equality, no subset tolerance; host-items-empty: refused apply reports blocked_downgrade (forceable, target-version-driven); host-items-empty: forceable refusal with zero lib/helper drift carries host_items: [] (present, empty); host-items-empty: forcing still applies (the skill-target downgrade); host-items-empty: forced apply touches no copy_lib/copy_helper items either.
